evals.name: shared-vs-mxe-auction
evals.prompt: I'm building a sealed-bid auction on Arcium. Bidders submit encrypted bids; the program keeps a running highest-bid+winner across bids; at close, reveal only the winner and winning price, losing bids stay secret. For (1) a submitted bid, (2) the running highest-bid state stored across computations, and (3) the final result, which encryption (Enc<Shared,T>, Enc<Mxe,T>, or .reveal()) for each, and what breaks if I use the wrong owner for the running state?
evals.expected_output: Maps bid input to Shared, persisted auction state to Mxe, public final result to reveal, and explains the confidentiality/silent-garbage failure mode.
expectations: Chooses Enc<Shared, T> for the submitted bid
Chooses Enc<Mxe, T> for the running highest-bid state
Chooses .reveal() for the final public result
Explains a wrong owner on the running state leaks aggregate state to one client's key and/or yields garbage when later decrypted with a different client key or nonce

evals.name: getting-started-terminology
evals.prompt: I'm new to Arcium. Explain what an MXE is, what an Arx node is, what a computation definition (comp def) is, and what Arcis is, then outline the minimal flow for my first app.
evals.expected_output: Defines the core terms correctly and gives the init/build/test/deploy shape for a first Arcium app.
expectations: Expands MXE as MPC eXecution Environment and describes it as the whole app (program + Arcis circuits + on-chain account)
Defines Arx node as an MPC node holding secret shares
Defines comp def as an on-chain account holding the circuit's compiled bytecode, initialized once per instruction
Defines Arcis as the Rust framework for confidential circuits

evals.name: scope-negative-token2022
evals.prompt: I want to add Token-2022 confidential transfers to my Solana program so token balances are hidden. How do I wire that up?
evals.expected_output: Routes the user away from Arcium-specific circuit guidance and toward Solana Token-2022 confidential transfers.
expectations: Identifies Token-2022 confidential transfers as Solana-native, not an Arcium/MPC use case
Does not force an Arcium circuit / queue_computation solution onto the task

evals.name: argbuilder-silent-failure-control
evals.prompt: My Arcium computation runs but the decrypted result is garbage. The circuit takes Enc<Shared, MyStruct>. My ArgBuilder is .plaintext_u128(nonce).encrypted_u64(ct). What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses missing Shared x25519 pubkey before nonce/ciphertext and classifies it as silent-failure wiring.
expectations: Identifies the missing .x25519_pubkey() call required for Enc<Shared, T>
States .x25519_pubkey() must precede the nonce and ciphertexts in ArgBuilder order
Frames it as a silent-failure class bug
